title: Black House (MMA)

infobox.value: [Ed Soares](/wiki/Ed_Soares "Ed Soares")    [Carlos Barreto](/wiki/Carlos_Barreto_(fighter) "Carlos Barreto (fighter)")   Jorge Guimarães   Rogério Camões
[Barb Honchak](/wiki/Barb_Honchak "Barb Honchak") Flyweight Champion (Invicta FC 2013-Present) 125 lb (57 kg; 8.9 st)

[Anderson Silva](/wiki/Anderson_Silva "Anderson Silva") Middleweight Champion (UFC 2006-2013) 185 lb (84 kg; 13.2 st)   [Antônio Rodrigo Nogueira](/wiki/Ant%C3%B4nio_Rodrigo_Nogueira "Antônio Rodrigo Nogueira") Interim Heavyweight Champion (UFC 2008) 240 lb (110 kg; 17 st), Heavyweight Champion (Pride 2001-2004) 210 lb (95 kg; 15 st)   [Rafael Cavalcante](/wiki/Rafael_Cavalcante "Rafael Cavalcante") Light Heavyweight Champion (Strikeforce 2010-2011) 205 lb (93 kg; 14.6 st)   [Lyoto Machida](/wiki/Lyoto_Machida "Lyoto Machida") Light Heavyweight Champion (UFC 2009-2010) 205 lb (93 kg; 14.6 st)   [Ronaldo Souza](/wiki/Ronaldo_Souza "Ronaldo Souza") Middleweight Champion (Strikeforce 2010-2011) 185 lb (84 kg; 13.2 st)
[Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil")  [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles"), [California](/wiki/California "California")

(Casa Preta) is a [mixed martial arts](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") fighting team and gym based out of [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil"). Opened on November 27, 2006, Black House was started by former [Brazilian Top Team](/wiki/Brazilian_Top_Team "Brazilian Top Team") member and black belt  [Carlos Barreto](/wiki/Carlos_Barreto_(fighter) "Carlos Barreto (fighter)"), Jorge Guimarães and Rogério Camões. Guimarães acted as the team's manager and Camões was in charge of the team's training.

[Black House inaugura em grande estilo :: TATAME ::](http://www.tatame.com.br/2006/11/29/Black-House-inaugura-em-grande-estilo) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20070717025828/http://www.tatame.com.br/2006/11/29/Black-House-inaugura-em-grande-estilo) July 17, 2007, at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine")
["Brazilian MMA expanding, exciting fights are on the way, July 25, 2007"](https://www.espn.com/extra/mma/news/story?id=2948970). [ESPN.com](/wiki/ESPN.com "ESPN.com"). 2006. Retrieved 2008-03-09.

["Anderson Silva talks UFC 79 & Black House, December 29, 2007"](https://web.archive.org/web/20080106114715/http://www.mmaweekly.com/absolutenm/templates/dailynews.asp?articleid=5360&zoneid=3). MMA Weekly. 2007. Archived from [the original](http://www.mmaweekly.com/absolutenm/templates/dailynews.asp?articleid=5360&zoneid=3) on January 6, 2008. Retrieved 2008-03-15.

Robb, Sharon (2008-05-04). ["UFC champions Nogueira, Silva partnering on fight academy in downtown Miami"](https://web.archive.org/web/20080616072705/http://www.sun-sentinel.com/sports/columnists/sfl-flsprobbmmacolly04sbmay04%2C0%2C2732324.column). South Florida Sun-Sentinel.com. Archived from [the original](http://www.sun-sentinel.com/sports/columnists/sfl-flsprobbmmacolly04sbmay04,0,2732324.column) on 2008-06-16. Retrieved 2008-06-14.
["Jordan Breen Show: Soares and Hirata"](http://www.sherdog.com/radio/Jordan-Breen-Show-Soares-and-Hirata-998). [Sherdog Radio](/wiki/Sherdog "Sherdog") (audio). 2009-04-23. Retrieved 2009-04-24.

["Black House reborn in Los Angeles"](https://web.archive.org/web/20081217012437/http://mmajunkie.com/news/13466/black-house-reborn-in-los-angeles.mma). John Morgan, MMA Junkie.com. Dec 13, 2008. Archived from [the original](http://mmajunkie.com/news/13466/black-house-reborn-in-los-angeles.mma) on 2008-12-17. Retrieved 2008-08-21.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

MMA training facility in Brazil

For other uses, see [Black House](/wiki/Black_House_(disambiguation) "Black House (disambiguation)").

Black House (Casa Preta)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Est. | 2006 |
| Founded by | [Ed Soares](/wiki/Ed_Soares "Ed Soares")    [Carlos Barreto](/wiki/Carlos_Barreto_(fighter) "Carlos Barreto (fighter)")   Jorge Guimarães   Rogério Camões |
| Primary trainers | Rogério Camões   Sylvio Behring |
| Current titleholders | [Barb Honchak](/wiki/Barb_Honchak "Barb Honchak") Flyweight Champion (Invicta FC 2013-Present) 125 lb (57 kg; 8.9 st) |
| Past titleholders | [Anderson Silva](/wiki/Anderson_Silva "Anderson Silva") Middleweight Champion (UFC 2006-2013) 185 lb (84 kg; 13.2 st)   [Antônio Rodrigo Nogueira](/wiki/Ant%C3%B4nio_Rodrigo_Nogueira "Antônio Rodrigo Nogueira") Interim Heavyweight Champion (UFC 2008) 240 lb (110 kg; 17 st), Heavyweight Champion (Pride 2001-2004) 210 lb (95 kg; 15 st)   [Rafael Cavalcante](/wiki/Rafael_Cavalcante "Rafael Cavalcante") Light Heavyweight Champion (Strikeforce 2010-2011) 205 lb (93 kg; 14.6 st)   [Lyoto Machida](/wiki/Lyoto_Machida "Lyoto Machida") Light Heavyweight Champion (UFC 2009-2010) 205 lb (93 kg; 14.6 st)   [Ronaldo Souza](/wiki/Ronaldo_Souza "Ronaldo Souza") Middleweight Champion (Strikeforce 2010-2011) 185 lb (84 kg; 13.2 st) |
| Training facilities | [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil")  [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles"), [California](/wiki/California "California") |
| Website | <http://www.blackhousemma.com/> |

**Black House** (Casa Preta) is a [mixed martial arts](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") fighting team and gym based out of [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil"). Opened on November 27, 2006, Black House was started by former [Brazilian Top Team](/wiki/Brazilian_Top_Team "Brazilian Top Team") member and black belt  [Carlos Barreto](/wiki/Carlos_Barreto_(fighter) "Carlos Barreto (fighter)"), Jorge Guimarães and Rogério Camões. Guimarães acted as the team's manager and Camões was in charge of the team's training.

Fighters on the team include [Anderson Silva](/wiki/Anderson_Silva "Anderson Silva"), [Lyoto Machida](/wiki/Lyoto_Machida "Lyoto Machida"), [Antônio Rogério Nogueira](/wiki/Ant%C3%B4nio_Rog%C3%A9rio_Nogueira "Antônio Rogério Nogueira"), [Antônio Rodrigo Nogueira](/wiki/Ant%C3%B4nio_Rodrigo_Nogueira "Antônio Rodrigo Nogueira"), [Pedro Rizzo](/wiki/Pedro_Rizzo "Pedro Rizzo"), [Erick Silva](/wiki/Erick_Silva "Erick Silva"), [Rafael Cavalcante](/wiki/Rafael_Cavalcante "Rafael Cavalcante") and former Chute Boxe Academy fighter [Antonio "Nino" Schembri](/wiki/Nino_%27Elvis%27_Schembri "Nino 'Elvis' Schembri"). The facility features an octagon, a boxing ring, and two dojos in the workout space.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-exciting-2)

## Re-branding and re-location

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Black_House_(MMA)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Re-branding and re-location")]

Despite the team's recent success in the UFC with the championships held by Silva, Machida and Nogueira, there had been much unrest due to non-competition related affairs. It appears that the team has moved or will be moving from their current location at X-Gym due to unresolvable disputes, and in a December 2007 interview Anderson Silva was quoted as stating that due to certain 'brand problems' the name Black House was likely to change following the move.[[3]](#cite_note-rebranding-3) On May 16, 2008, Silva and [Antônio Rodrigo Nogueira](/wiki/Ant%C3%B4nio_Rodrigo_Nogueira "Antônio Rodrigo Nogueira") opened the Nogueira and Silva Mixed Martial Arts Academy in [Miami](/wiki/Miami "Miami"), [Florida](/wiki/Florida "Florida").[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Despite these events, in a 2009 interview with [Sherdog Radio](/wiki/Sherdog "Sherdog") host Jordan Breen, Black House manager [Ed Soares](/wiki/Ed_Soares "Ed Soares") indicated that Black House may open a new training location in Los Angeles, California. He also shed light on some of the issues that led many to believe that the team had disbanded, most notably the team management's falling out with the owners of X-Gym, the original location and headquarters for their training camps.[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Notable Fighters

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Black_House_(MMA)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Notable Fighters")]

* [Anderson Silva](/wiki/Anderson_Silva "Anderson Silva") (Former [UFC Middleweight Champion](/wiki/UFC_Middleweight_Championship "UFC Middleweight Championship"))
* [Junior dos Santos](/wiki/Junior_dos_Santos "Junior dos Santos") (Former [UFC Heavyweight Champion](/wiki/UFC_Heavyweight_Championship "UFC Heavyweight Championship"))
* [José Aldo](/wiki/Jos%C3%A9_Aldo "José Aldo") (Former UFC Featherweight Champion)
* [Rafael "Feijao" Cavalcante](/wiki/Rafael_Cavalcante "Rafael Cavalcante") (Former [Strikeforce](/wiki/Strikeforce_(mixed_martial_arts) "Strikeforce (mixed martial arts)") Light Heavyweight Champion)
* [Antônio Rodrigo Nogueira](/wiki/Ant%C3%B4nio_Rodrigo_Nogueira "Antônio Rodrigo Nogueira") (Former [Pride FC](/wiki/PRIDE_Fighting_Championships "PRIDE Fighting Championships") heavyweight champion and former [UFC Interim Heavyweight Champion](/wiki/UFC_Heavyweight_Championship "UFC Heavyweight Championship"))
* [Lyoto Machida](/wiki/Lyoto_Machida "Lyoto Machida") (Former [UFC Light Heavyweight Champion](/wiki/UFC_Light_Heavyweight_Championship "UFC Light Heavyweight Championship"))
* [Antônio Rogério Nogueira](/wiki/Ant%C3%B4nio_Rog%C3%A9rio_Nogueira "Antônio Rogério Nogueira") (2006 and 2007 Brazilian Super Heavyweight Boxing Champion and 2007 Pan American Games bronze medalist)
* Mario Miranda (former [M-1 Middleweight Champion](/wiki/M-1_Global "M-1 Global"))
* [Ronaldo "Jacaré" Souza](/wiki/Ronaldo_Souza "Ronaldo Souza") (Former [Strikeforce](/wiki/Strikeforce_(mixed_martial_arts) "Strikeforce (mixed martial arts)") Middleweight Champion)
* [Pedro Rizzo](/wiki/Pedro_Rizzo "Pedro Rizzo") (former World Vale Tudo Championship (WVC) heavyweight champion)
* [Andre Galvao](/wiki/Andre_Galvao "Andre Galvao") (multiple time World Jiu-Jitsu Champion and Pan American Champion)
* [Thales Leites](/wiki/Thales_Leites "Thales Leites") (Current Superior Challenge middleweight champion)
* [Satoshi Ishii](/wiki/Satoshi_Ishii "Satoshi Ishii") (Olympic Gold Medalist in Judo)
* [Carlos Eduardo Rocha](/wiki/Carlos_Eduardo_Rocha "Carlos Eduardo Rocha") (Free Fight Alliance Middleweight Champion and La Onda Manto Cup Champion)
* [Roger Gracie](/wiki/Roger_Gracie "Roger Gracie")[[8]](#cite_note-8) (multiple time ADCC Gold Medalist and World Jiu-Jitsu Champion)
* [Rafael dos Anjos](/wiki/Rafael_dos_Anjos "Rafael dos Anjos") (former UFC Lightweight Champion, Former Fury FC Lightweight Championship)
* [Glover Teixeira](/wiki/Glover_Teixeira "Glover Teixeira") (former member of the Brazilian National Wrestling Team)
